title: Processing of Pacbio Iso-seq sequences
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"1mkim","text":"This protocol is to further process the sequences generated from ICE and Quiver.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]}],"entityMap":{}}
guidelines: {"blocks":[{"key":"en07i","text":"Remove of the detected contaminant sequences","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":44}],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"81hf0","text":"If X \u003e Y, then the sequences were removed. This is done subsequently after each detecting steps, therefore, the dataset number is decreased and its name has changed from step 4 onwards.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]}],"entityMap":{}}
before_start: {"blocks":[{"key":"5viqh","text":"Raw data from Pacbio Iso-Seq needs to be processed with RS IsoSeq (version 2.3) pipeline.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]}],"entityMap":{}}
steps: To remove the Primer IIA sequence motifs used in library preparations.
LQ output or non-full length coverage sequences may from rare transcripts or lower coverage sequences. HQ: high quality sequences, LQ, low quality sequences generated from RS IsoSeq pipeline
To further remove the redundant sequences. The output dataset was hereafter called dataset A
BLASTn (1e-10) the dataset A against the complete C.arabica chloroplast genome. Accession number: EF044213.1 (processed with CLC genomic workbench)
BLASTn (1e-10) the dataset B against the N.tabacum and V. vinifera complete mitochondrial genomes (relate species) Accession number: BA000042.1 and FM179380.1 (processed with CLC genomic workbench)

BLASTn (1e-10) the dataset C against the public available ribosomal genes from C. arabica, C.canephora and C.eugenioides Accession number: AJ224846, EU650386, DQ153609, AF416459, EU650384, EU650385, AF542981, AF542990, JX459583, JX459584, JX459585, JX459586, JX459587, DQ153593, AF542982, DQ423064, DQ153588, DQ153621, AF542986 (processed with CLC genomic workbench)
BLASTn (1e-10) the dataset D against the reference genomes of virus and viroid Download from NCBI (processed with CLC genomic workbench)
BLASTn (1e-10) the dataset E against the reference genome of prokaryotes Download from NCBI (processed with CLC genomic workbench)
BLASTx (1e-10) the dataset F against the fungal proteins Download from NCBI (processed with CLC genomic workbench)
Significant matches are filtered with bit score (X) ≥ 300 and identity ≥ 80% Processed with CLC genomic workbench
All the significant matches were confirmed with cloud BLASTn (bit score (Y)) Processed with CLC genomic workbench

If X > Y, then the sequences should be removed. This is done subsequently after each detecting steps, therefore, the dataset number is decreased and its name has changed from step 4 onwards. Processed with CLC genomic workbench
Sequence quality was then accessed with the Fasta Statistics through Galaxy/GVL 4.0 Hereafter the dataset was called dataset G
authors: Bing Cheng; Agnelo Furtado; Robert Henry
creator: Robert J Henry
